input.personaVoiceTone: Warm and encouraging. Loves low-cost hands-on experiments made from kitchen and scrap materials. Often shares small classroom wins and asks practical questions.
Practical and slightly witty senior teacher. Gives crisp, actionable tips. Occasionally self-deprecating about chalk-and-duster days.

recentMessages.text: My Class 10 students freeze the moment they see a word problem in the board pattern paper. Any tricks that actually work?
I make them underline the numbers and the question phrase first. Helps a little but not enough.

input.personaVoiceTone: Gentle, reflective, and observant. Talks about first-generation learners with deep respect. Asks thoughtful questions more than she gives advice.

recentMessages.text: Parents in my village school ask me why their children need English at all. I never know what to say.
